summary_one_line: Input must contain a valid OpenType font directory carrying a 'GSUB' or 'GPOS' four-byte table tag so harfbuzz reaches GSUBGPOS::get_script_list() with a non-version-1/2 major version.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must embed the 4-byte ASCII table tag 'GSUB' (47 53 55 42) or 'GPOS' (47 50 4f 53) at the position where harfbuzz's font-directory parser equality-compares against those tag literals, so that the parser walks into OT::GSUBGPOS::get_script_list() and the switch on u.version.major falls through case 1 (and case 2) into the default arm returning Null(ScriptList).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the literal four-byte tag at offsets 0x1c-0x1f — six show 'GSUB' (47 53 55 42), four show 'GPOS' (47 50 4f 53) — together with an OpenType-like header prefix (00 01 00 00 sfnt_version) at offsets 0x00-0x03, which is what walks the parser into the GSUBGPOS table.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: None of the five loser-blocking seeds contain the 'GSUB' or 'GPOS' four-byte literal at any concentrated offset — at offsets 0x1c-0x1f the loser byte sets are scattered (e.g. {00, 1a, 56, 0c, ...}, {00, 20, 55, 0c, ...}) with no occurrences of the 47 53 55 42 / 47 50 4f 53 ASCII run, so the font-directory walk never reaches the GSUBGPOS dispatch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog intercept the 4-byte memcmp / integer-CMP that harfbuzz uses to match the table tag during directory lookup, record the 'GSUB'/'GPOS' literal in the CmpLogObserver buffer, and the I2SRandReplace mutator substitutes that literal into the candidate input at the matching byte offsets in a single step. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace; naive must synthesize the four-byte ASCII tag through random havoc (probability ~1/2^32 per attempt), and value_profile's per-byte distance feedback gives at best a per-byte nudge that did not bridge the four-byte literal within the 12-hour budget.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs at this branch share the I2S technique delta and are unanimous (10/10 winner-resolved vs 10/10 loser-blocked).
value_profile_cmplog>value_profile is also under the I2S delta (vpc adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile).
The blocker is the default arm of a switch on u.version.major in GSUBGPOS::get_script_list().
Reaching the default arm requires walking into GSUBGPOS, which is only invoked when the parser identifies a GSUB or GPOS table in the font directory.
Winner seed 1 carries the literal 'GSUB' ASCII run at offsets 0x1c-0x1f.
Winner seed 2 also carries 'GSUB' at offsets 0x1c-0x1f.
Winner seed 3 carries 'GPOS' at offsets 0x1c-0x1f, confirming the tag is not unique to GSUB but is the 4-byte literal family the gate looks for.
Winner seeds also share an OpenType-style sfnt header prefix 00 01 00 00 at offsets 0x00-0x03.
At offset 0x1c the winner byte set is concentrated on 0x47 ('G') ten out of ten while the loser byte set is scattered without the 'G' anchor.
Offsets 0x1d/0x1e/0x1f split cleanly between 'SUB' and 'POS' on the winner side, again concentrated, while losers are scattered with no occurrences of the tag.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of naive's mutator stack, scanning the input for byte sequences matching one operand of an intercepted CMP and substituting the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive only has the havoc + token stack and has no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile also lacks I2SRandReplace; it only adds the CMP_MAP gradient on top of naive's mutator stack.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of the value_profile feedback stack.
The dash-row I2S-floor signal appears in the ancestor chain of winning seed 00078a6e395420d4 — confirmed by db_query.py lineage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
their
presence in a winning seed's ancestor chain is direct (lower-bound)
evidence of I2S contribution.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 9107 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 00078a6e395420d4` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 22 of a 23-level chain (ancestor e0a5b09f80625fb6). Corroborated with seed 0009a8dcb2f65ed7 which has a dash row at depth 32 of a 33-level chain at the SAME ancestor e0a5b09f80625fb6 — both winning seeds share an I2SRandReplace-discovered root, direct evidence that cmplog's I2S substitution introduced the 'GSUB'/'GPOS' tag literal into the lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that switches on a 4-byte field equality-compared against two literal tags ('GSUB','GPOS') and gates trap on the default arm — if cmplog and value_profile_cmplog FAIL to reach the trap while naive/value_profile succeed at comparable rate, the I2S-substitution attribution is refuted.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact dataflow from input byte offset 0x1c-0x1f to harfbuzz's tag-CMP site is inferred from byte-set concentration; the actual harfbuzz directory parser may apply length/checksum gating before the tag CMP, in which case the I2S substitution alone would not be sufficient.
